mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines below 8.0% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved earnings outlooks over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for 5-day horizon, or 5-day returns in interaction cases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.0 signals improving manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO declines below 101.0 in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL creates a disinflationary environment that supports equity valuations through lower real rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 for 20d horizon, or 20d returns in combined FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP (GDPC1) below 11500.0 billion dollars signals recession risks that depress equity prices through earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low GDP regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or GDP rises above 11550.0 in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 135,000 jobs combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 8.0% indicates a tightening labor market that drives inflationary pressures and negative equity returns through rate hike expectations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for 5-day horizon, or 5-day returns in interaction cases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 101.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low INDPRO regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO rises above 101.5 in 1 month.
